items.body:
## Summary

Seven packages have failing CI check runs on the `main` branch at commit [`734bf6b96b`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/734bf6b96b51337df9e5d64cbe8020fa07b14779) (2026-03-10T07:15:13Z). The most critical failure is `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence` (126 playback unit test failures) which has a clear, reproducible code bug. The remaining failures are live-service or CI-infrastructure issues.

---

## Failures

### `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence` (`sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest`)

**Failing tests (sample — all 126 playback tests fail with the same error):**
- `DocumentIntelligenceClient > content analysis > pdf file stream` in `test/public/node/analysis.spec.ts`
- `DocumentIntelligenceClient > content analysis > png file stream` in `test/public/node/analysis.spec.ts`
- `DocumentIntelligenceClient > content analysis > jpeg file stream` in `test/public/node/analysis.spec.ts`
- `DocumentIntelligenceClient > content analysis > tiff file stream` in `test/public/node/analysis.spec.ts`
- (+ 122 more across all `*.spec.ts` node test files)

**Check run IDs:** `66422913994`, `66422788449`, `66421430703`, `66421185625`, `66421164425`

**Error:**
```
Unable to find a record for the request
  POST (endpoint/redacted)
Uri doesn't match:
  request ((endpoint/redacted)
  record  ((endpoint/redacted)
```

**Root cause:**
The playback `ENDPOINT` constant in `test/utils/constants.ts` is `"(endpoint/redacted) (trailing slash). The client factory in `src/documentIntelligence.ts` appends `/documentintelligence` to the endpoint parameter:

```typescript
// src/documentIntelligence.ts, line 28-29
const endpointUrl =
  options.endpoint ?? options.baseUrl ?? `\$\{endpointParam}/documentintelligence`;
```

`"(endpoint/redacted) + `"/documentintelligence"` → `"(endpoint/redacted) (double slash).

The test recordings (assets tag `js/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest_557bf0ed19`) store URLs with a **single** slash (`(endpoint/redacted), so every playback request mismatches every recording.

**Suggested fix (Option A — fix the test constant, minimal change):**

```diff
--- a/sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest/test/utils/constants.ts
+++ b/sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest/test/utils/constants.ts
@@ -32,7 +32,7 @@ export const EnvVarKeys = {
   TEST_MODE: "TEST_MODE",
 } as const;
 
-export const ENDPOINT = "(endpoint/redacted)
+export const ENDPOINT = "(endpoint/redacted)";
 export const KEY = "api_key";
 export const DISABLE_LOCAL_AUTH = false;
```

**Suggested fix (Option B — defensive fix in source, also fixes live/record mode when Azure management API returns endpoints with trailing slashes):**

````diff
--- a/sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest/src/documentIntelligence.ts
+++ b/sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest/src/documentIntelligence.ts
@@ -26,7 +26,7 @@ export default function createClient(
 ): DocumentIntelligenceClient {
   const endpointUrl =
-    options.endpoint ?? options.baseUrl ?? `\$\{endpointParam}/documentintelligence`;
+    options.endpoint ?? options.baseUrl ?? `\$\{endpointParam.replace(/\/$/, "")}/documentintelligence`;
```

Option B is preferred as it is more defensive and handles the case where the Azure management plane (`CognitiveServicesManagementClient.accounts.get(…).properties.endpoint`) returns an endpoint with a trailing slash in live/record mode.

**Note:** `sdk/formrecognizer/ai-form-recognizer/test/utils/constants.ts` has the same `"(endpoint/redacted) constant; its client factory should be audited for the same trailing-slash concatenation.

**Reproduced locally:** Unable to reproduce (pnpm install fails due to Azure DevOps feed authentication requirements in CI), but the root cause is unambiguous from reading the source code and recordings.

---

### `@azure/attestation` (`sdk/attestation/attestation`)

**Failing test:**
- `AttestationClient in Browser > #attestTpm` in `test/browser/attestationTests.browser.spec.ts`

**Check run IDs:** `66434853540`, `66434806065`

**Error:**
```
A VBS attestation policy has not been set on the attestation provider.
```

**Root cause:**
This is a **live service test** failure. The test attempts to perform TPM attestation, which requires a VBS (Virtualization Based Security) policy to be pre-configured on the test attestation provider. The CI test environment's provider either lacks this policy or the policy was reset. This is a service/environment configuration issue, not a code bug.

**Suggested fix:** Re-configure the VBS attestation policy on the attestation provider used by CI. No code change required.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Attestation service)

---

### `@azure/digital-twins-core` (`sdk/digitaltwins/digital-twins-core`)

**Check run IDs:** `66431595854`, `66429657836`

**Error:**
```
Unhandled: Not found workingDirectory: /Users/runner/work/1/s/$(PackagePath)
```

**Root cause:**
This is a **CI pipeline infrastructure failure**. The Azure Pipelines variable `$(PackagePath)` was not expanded, meaning the pipeline YAML has a misconfigured step that references an undefined variable. Notably, the test output reports **0 actual test failures** (444 passed, 6 "other"/skipped) — all tests passed; the pipeline step itself errored after tests completed.

**Suggested fix:** Investigate the pipeline YAML for the `digitaltwins` service directory for a step that uses `$(PackagePath)` without it being defined, or update the pipeline template to handle this case.

**Reproduced locally:** No (CI pipeline infrastructure issue)

---

### `@azure/storage-blob` (`sdk/storage/storage-blob`)

**Check run IDs:** `66429792013`, `66429608538`, `66428096672`

**Failing tests (sample):**
- `BlobServiceClient > createContainer and deleteContainer`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlockFromURL copy source blob as single block`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlock, stageBlockURL and commitBlockList with CPK`

**Error:**
```
Public access is not permitted on this storage account.
RequestId:63ed5623-501e-0035-6c6b-b021d0000000
```

**Root cause:**
Live service configuration: the storage account used by CI has public access disabled, but the test scenarios assume or require it. This is a test environment configuration issue.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Storage account)

---

### `@azure/storage-file-share` (`sdk/storage/storage-file-share`)

**Check run IDs:** `66431474845`, `66431471793`

**Failing test:**
- `ContentChecksumValidation with client config - CRC64 > uploadFile should success for large data`

**Error:**
```
The Structured Message was not valid.
RequestId:239de779-001a-0007-0f6f-b021a7000000
Time:2026-03-10T09:26:03.5957057Z
```

**Root cause:**
Live service error returned by the Azure Files service. "Structured Message" validation failures are service-side errors. This is a transient or environment-specific service issue.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Storage account)

---

### `@azure/communication-identity` (`sdk/communication/communication-identity`)

**Check run IDs:** `66431438792`, `66431427233`, `66431129833`, `66428780741`, `66428211779`, `66428169760`, `66428020248`

**Failing test:**
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] [AAD]`

**Error:**
```
invalid_grant: Error(s): 5000224 - Timestamp: 2026-03-10 09:14:02Z
AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available. If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: d2b6c090-b1bb-4b18-9dec-ff548fed0e00
```

**Root cause:**
Azure Active Directory is returning a service availability error (`AADSTS5000224`). This is a transient AAD service issue affecting the test's ability to acquire tokens.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live AAD / communication identity service)

---

### `@azure/communication-short-codes` (`sdk/communication/communication-short-codes`)

**Check run IDs:** `66429615660`, `66429610540`, `66429425507`, `66429412976`, `66429109826`, `66427150345`, `66427052015`, `66426977125`, `66426908265`

**Failing test:**
- `ShortCodesClient - manage Attachments > can manage Attachments`

**Error:**
```
The server encountered an internal error.
````

**Root cause:**
The Communication Short Codes service returned HTTP 500 (Internal Server Error). This is a transient server-side error.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Communication service)

---

## Additional Notes

- **Only `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence` has an actionable code fix.** All other failures are live-service, transient, or CI environment issues that require service team or infra attention.
- The double-slash URL bug in `ai-document-intelligence-rest` affects **126 out of 135** playback tests (93% failure rate) across all OS platforms (Windows, Ubuntu, macOS).
- Option B for the fix (`endpointParam.replace(/\/$/, "")`) is safer because Azure management plane API (`account.properties?.endpoint`) typically returns endpoints with trailing slashes in live scenarios. Option A only fixes playback.
- Check `sdk/formrecognizer/ai-form-recognizer` for the same `ENDPOINT = "(endpoint/redacted) pattern — it may have the same latent issue if its client factory also appends a path segment.
